heading: Grants to develop and establish State infrastructures to support working individuals with disabilities
label: 1320b–22 Grants to develop and establish State infrastructures to support working individuals with disabilities
notes: References in TextSection 101(a) of this Act, referred to in subsec. (d), is section 101(a) of the Ticket to Work and Work Incentives Improvement Act of 1999, , which enacted .Pub. L. 106–170section 1320b–19 of this titleSection 101(f) of this Act, referred to in subsec. (f), is section 101(f) of the Ticket to Work and Work Incentives Improvement Act of 1999, , which is set out as a note under .Pub. L. 106–170section 1320b–19 of this title
CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Ticket to Work and Work Incentives Improvement Act of 1999, and not as part of the Social Security Act which comprises this chapter.

Change of NameCommittee on Commerce of House of Representatives changed to Committee on Energy and Commerce of House of Representatives, and jurisdiction over matters relating to securities and exchanges and insurance generally transferred to Committee on Financial Services of House of Representatives by House Resolution No. 5, One Hundred Seventh Congress, .Jan. 3, 2001

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary of Health and Human Services (in this section referred to as the “Secretary”) shall award grants described in subsection (b) to States to support the design, establishment, and operation of State infrastructures that provide items and services to support working individuals with disabilities.

children.heading: Application
children.label: 2 Application
children.text: In order to be eligible for an award of a grant under this section, a State shall submit an application to the Secretary at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary shall require.

children.heading: Definition of State
children.label: 3 Definition of State
children.text: In this section, the term “State” means each of the 50 States, the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, Guam, the United States Virgin Islands, American Samoa, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.

children.heading: Grants for infrastructure and outreach
children.label: b Grants for infrastructure and outreach
A
children.text: support the establishment, implementation, and operation of the State infrastructures described in subsection (a); and

children.label: B
children.text: conduct outreach campaigns regarding the existence of such infrastructures.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No State may receive a grant under this subsection unless the State demonstrates to the satisfaction of the Secretary that the State makes personal assistance services available under the State plan under subchapter XIX of this chapter to the extent necessary to enable individuals with disabilities to remain employed, including individuals described in if the State has elected to provide medical assistance under such plan to such individuals.section 1396a(a)(10)(A)(ii)(XIII) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: earning at least the applicable minimum wage requirement under and working at least 40 hours per month; orsection 206 of title 29

children.label: II
children.text: being engaged in a work effort that meets substantial and reasonable threshold criteria for hours of work, wages, or other measures, as defined and approved by the Secretary.

children.heading: Personal assistance services
children.label: ii Personal assistance services
children.text: The term “personal assistance services” means a range of services, provided by 1 or more persons, designed to assist an individual with a disability to perform daily activities on and off the job that the individual would typically perform if the individual did not have a disability. Such services shall be designed to increase the individual’s control in life and ability to perform everyday activities on or off the job.

children.label: i
children.text: rewards States for their efforts in encouraging individuals described in paragraph (2)(A) to be employed; and

children.label: ii
children.text: does not provide a State that has not elected to provide medical assistance under subchapter XIX of this chapter to individuals described in with proportionally more funds for a fiscal year than a State that has exercised such election.section 1396a(a)(10)(A)(ii)(XIII) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Subject to subclause (II), no State with an approved application under this section shall receive a grant for a fiscal year that is less than $500,000.

children.heading: Pro rata reductions
children.label: II Pro rata reductions
children.text: If the funds appropriated under subsection (e) for a fiscal year are not sufficient to pay each State with an application approved under this section the minimum amount described in subclause (I), the Secretary shall pay each such State an amount equal to the pro rata share of the amount made available.

children.heading: States that elected optional medicaid eligibility
children.label: I States that elected optional medicaid eligibility
children.text: No State that has an application that has been approved under this section and that has elected to provide medical assistance under subchapter XIX of this chapter to individuals described in shall receive a grant for a fiscal year that exceeds 10 percent of the total expenditures by the State (including the reimbursed Federal share of such expenditures) for medical assistance provided under such subchapter for such individuals, as estimated by the State and approved by the Secretary.section 1396a(a)(10)(A)(ii)(XIII) of this title

children.heading: Other States
children.label: II Other States
children.text: The Secretary shall determine, consistent with the limit described in subclause (I), a maximum award limit for a grant for a fiscal year for a State that has an application that has been approved under this section but that has not elected to provide medical assistance under subchapter XIX of this chapter to individuals described in .section 1396a(a)(10)(A)(ii)(XIII) of this title

children.heading: Funds awarded to States
children.label: 1 Funds awarded to States
children.text: Funds awarded to a State under a grant made under this section for a fiscal year shall remain available until expended.

children.heading: Funds not awarded to States
children.label: 2 Funds not awarded to States
children.text: Funds not awarded to States in the fiscal year for which they are appropriated shall remain available in succeeding fiscal years for awarding by the Secretary.

children.heading: Annual report
children.label: d Annual report
children.text: A State that is awarded a grant under this section shall submit an annual report to the Secretary on the use of funds provided under the grant. Each report shall include the percentage increase in the number of title II disability beneficiaries, as defined in (as added by section 101(a) of this Act) in the State, and title XVI disability beneficiaries, as defined in (as so added) in the State who return to work.section 1320b–19(k)(3) of this titlesection 1320b–19(k)(4) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: for fiscal year 2001, $20,000,000;

children.label: B
children.text: for fiscal year 2002, $25,000,000;

children.label: C
children.text: for fiscal year 2003, $30,000,000;

children.label: D
children.text: for fiscal year 2004, $35,000,000;

children.label: E
children.text: for fiscal year 2005, $40,000,000; and

children.label: F
children.text: for each of fiscal years 2006 through 2011, the amount appropriated for the preceding fiscal year increased by the percentage increase (if any) in the Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (United States city average) for the preceding fiscal year.

children.heading: Budget authority
children.label: 2 Budget authority
children.text: This subsection constitutes budget authority in advance of appropriations Acts and represents the obligation of the Federal Government to provide for the payment of the amounts appropriated under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Recommendation
children.label: f Recommendation
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary, in consultation with the Ticket to Work and Work Incentives Advisory Panel established by section 101(f) of this Act, shall submit a recommendation to the Committee on Commerce of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Finance of the Senate regarding whether the grant program established under this section should be continued after fiscal year 2011.October 1, 2010
